comments.author: @batmannbg
comments.text: REALLY?  not one natural wonder from Serbia in this list???shame on you!

comments.author: @hope_is_immortal_thing
comments.text: And not a single place from the Baltic states? Latvia, Lithuania and Estonia have places that 100% deserve to be in this list. I feel disappointed.

replies.author: @hope_is_immortal_thing
replies.text: ​​@cityofolmecsas a proud Latvian I deffinitelly agree with you 👍 and bot only capitals are beautiful. We have many beautiful cities and also nature.

replies.author: @cityofolmecs
replies.text: I agree.  Riga, Tallin and Vilnius historical architecture is amazing as well as the cultural background. Not to mention the thousand lakes and respective buildings marshlands and snowing scenery. Good people that love mushrooms, opera, forests and hot baths live there.

replies.author: @hope_is_immortal_thing
replies.text: ​@TanjaHermannare you sure? This is still introduction of the video, not like 1 of 100. Nobody will ever learn that this is it.

comments.author: @wondertv815
comments.text: This video captures that rare balance of beauty and mystery, making me want to pack a bag and explore the unknown corners of the planet.

comments.author: @bring_marc_the_horizon
comments.text: We are so lucky to live in Europe and have all that incredible nature, architecture, history, art, music, food, culture and traditions to call our own! ❤🇪🇺

comments.author: @dietandfitness1086
comments.text: This video brought back memories 😭 I did Sorrento, Positano, Capri and Pompeii last year and honestly the Amalfi Coast is even more magical in person. What helped me most before the trip was reading Wonders Along the Amalfi Coast by Daniel Crosswell because it explained transport, ferry routes, food spots and day-trip planning in a really beginner-friendly way.
